zh.lifeSummary: 當Count Henry de Sneyde of  Tunstall and Stafford於1270年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，他的父親Count William de Sneyde20歲，母親Matilda18歲。他和Margaret de Tunstall至少有1個兒子。他去世於1350年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾彤絲朵 白 阿德巴斯頓享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sneyde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Sneyd in Burslem (Staffordshire), The Sneyd near Bloxwich (also Staffordshire), or Snead Farm and Snead Common in Rock (Worcestershire), all of which derived from Middle English snede, Old English snǣd ‘detached piece of land or woodland’. Compare Snaith .

variants.name: Snaith
meanings: English: habitational name from Snaith in Yorkshire. The placename derives from Old Norse sneith ‘slice of land, piece of land cut off’.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Count Henry de Sneyde of  Tunstall and Stafford was born in 1270, in Staffordshire, England, his father, Count William de Sneyde, was 20 and his mother, Matilda, was 18. He had at least 1 son with Margaret de Tunstall. He died in 1350, in Tunstall by Adbaston, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 80.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret de Tunstall. Il est décédé en 1350, à Tunstall by Adbaston, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 80 ans.
